title: Behavioral: tech leadership + project deep-dive
focus: Walk through your hardest ML system. Trade-offs you considered AND rejected.

items.label: Project deep-dive: structure & narrative
interviewQuestions: Walk me through the most complex ML system you've shipped.
What were the trade-offs you considered, and what did you NOT build?

items.label: Influence: convincing leadership of an ML investment
interviewQuestions: Tell me about a time you convinced leadership to invest in an ML platform.
Describe a time a senior leader pushed back on your proposal — how did you handle it?

items.label: Cross-team / cross-org collaboration
interviewQuestions: Describe a project that required deep alignment across teams — what did you do?
Tell me about a time another team's incentives conflicted with yours, and how you got to a shared outcome.

tracks.label: Behavioral · Technical deep-dive for senior ML roles

items.label: Project deep dive: decisions rejected, incidents handled, and architecture trade-offs defended
interviewQuestions: Walk through the hardest ML system you owned and the alternatives you rejected.
What did you measure before launch, what surprised you after launch, and what did you change?
How did you communicate a technical trade-off to product, infra, and leadership stakeholders?

items.label: Company-specific narrative: why this team, why this product, why now
interviewQuestions: How do you adapt your project story for Meta, Google, OpenAI, Amazon, and a startup?
Which parts of your story prove end-to-end ownership instead of narrow model work?
How do you discuss failures without sounding defensive or vague?
